---
title: "Replicate_2011"
format: html
---

# Packages

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(dsims)
library(Distance)
library(dssd)
library(dsm)
library(sf)
library(mgcv)
library(qrng) 
library(furrr)
library(future)
```

# Import Functions

```{r}
source(here::here("Functions4.R"))
```

# Constants

```{r}
true_N <- 3000

points_or_lines = "line"

trunc_dist <- 40
scale_parameter <- 15
design_spacing <- 2000

density_grid_spacing <- 100



outer_loop_reps <- 10
bootstrap_reps <- 20
```

# Code

## Region

```{r}
# region <- make_region(region_sf = NULL, 0, 5000, 0, 5000, "m")
# plot(region, main = "Survey Region")
```

# Replicating Fewster Triangle regions

```{r}
# Define a right triangle with 1000m axes
triangle_coords <- matrix(c(
  10000, 10000, 
  10000, 0, 
  0, 0, 
  10000, 10000       
), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)

region <- st_polygon(list(triangle_coords)) |> 
  st_sfc() |> 
  st_sf(geometry = _)

region <- make_region(region_sf = region)

plot(region)
```

## Density

```{r}
# my_hotspots <- list(
#   list(centre = c(2000, 8000), sigma = 800, amplitude = 1.2),
#   list(centre = c(5000, 2000), sigma = 6000, amplitude = 0.5),
#   list(centre = c(8000, 9000), sigma = 1000, amplitude = 2)
# )

my_hotspots1 <- list(
  list(centre = c(5000, 5000), sigma = 80000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true1 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots1)



my_hotspots2 <- list(
  list(centre = c(0, 0), sigma = 10000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true2 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots2)



my_hotspots3 <- list(
  list(centre = c(10000, 0), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 2500), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 5000), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 7500), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 10000), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true3 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots3)



my_hotspots4 <- list(
  list(centre = c(10000, 0), sigma = 10000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true4 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots4)

densities <- list(density_true1, density_true2, density_true3, density_true4)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  # Assuming the first list element contains the sf density grid
  geom_sf(data = density_true4@density.surface[[1]], aes(fill = density), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  geom_sf(data = region@region, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal()
```


# Test Survey

```{r, message=FALSE}
test_region <- region

test_density_true <- density_true4

test_sim_truth <- generate_simulated_pop(
  region = test_region,
  density_true = test_density_true,
  N = true_N,
  scale_param = scale_parameter,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

test_population_description <- test_sim_truth$population_description
test_detectability <- test_sim_truth$detectability

test_survey <- survey_data(
  region = region,
  realized_population = test_sim_truth$population,
  transect_type = points_or_lines,
  spacing = design_spacing,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

test_ds <- fit_ds(
  region = test_region,
  dist_data = test_survey$dist_data,
  obsdata = test_survey$obsdata,
  segdata = test_survey$segdata,
  transect_type = points_or_lines,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

test_design <- test_survey$design

test_analyses <- make.ds.analysis(dfmodel = ~ 1,
                                  key = "hn",
                                  truncation = trunc_dist,
                                  criteria = "AIC")

# test_sim <- make.simulation(reps = 3,
#                             design = test_design,
#                             population.description = test_population_description,
#                             detectability = test_detectability,
#                             ds.analysis = test_analyses)
# 
# # Generate a single instance of a survey: a population, set of transects
# # and the resulting distance data
# test_survey <- run.survey(test_sim)

# 1. Extract the spatial objects and distance data from your custom lists
pop_df <- test_sim_truth$population@population 
trans_sf <- test_survey$transects@samplers
obs_df <- test_survey$dist_data

# 2. Flag detected animals AND convert the raw x/y coordinates to spatial sf points
pop_sf <- pop_df |> 
  mutate(Status = if_else(as.character(individual) %in% as.character(obs_df$object), 
                          "Detected", "Missed")) |> 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), crs = st_crs(test_region@region)) # Convert to sf for ggplot

# 3. Build the plot
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = test_region@region, fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_sf(data = trans_sf, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_sf(data = pop_sf, aes(color = Status), size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Detected" = "red", "Missed" = "grey80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated Survey with Detections",
    subtitle = paste("Detected:", nrow(obs_df), "of", nrow(pop_sf)),
    color = "Animal Status"
  )

# plot(test_ds$ds_model)

# sum(test_ds$ds_model$dht$individuals$Nhat.by.sample$n)

# test_ds$ds_model$dht$individuals$summary$n
```


## For each region:

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
num_cores <- max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 2)
plan(multisession, workers = num_cores)

num_scenarios <- length(densities)

message(sprintf("Starting parallel simulation across %d workers...", num_cores))

all_runs <- future_map(1:outer_loop_reps, function(b) {

  inner_results <- map(seq_along(densities), function(i) {

    sim_pop <- generate_simulated_pop(
      region = region,
      density_true = densities[[i]]
    )

    survey_results <- survey_data(
      region = region,
      realized_population = sim_pop$population,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      spacing = design_spacing,
      truncation = trunc_dist
    )

    ds_results <- fit_ds(
      region = region,
      dist_data = survey_results$dist_data,
      obsdata = survey_results$obsdata,
      segdata = survey_results$segdata,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      truncation = trunc_dist
    )

    dsm_results <- fit_dsm_and_surface(
      obsdata = survey_results$obsdata,
      segdata = survey_results$segdata,
      ds_model = ds_results$ds_model,
      region = region,
      N_hat = ds_results$N_hat,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      x_space = density_grid_spacing,
      y_space = density_grid_spacing
    )

    spatial_variances <- striplet_boxlet(
      region = region,
      segdata = survey_results$segdata,
      dsm_model = dsm_results$dsm,
      dsm_surface = dsm_results$density_surface,
      ds_results = ds_results,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      spacing = design_spacing,
      truncation = trunc_dist
    )

    if (points_or_lines == "line") {

      R2_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_R2 %||% NA_real_
      R3_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_R3 %||% NA_real_
      S1_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_S1 %||% NA_real_
      S2_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_S2 %||% NA_real_
      O1_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_O1 %||% NA_real_
      O2_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_O2 %||% NA_real_

      P2_est <- NA_real_
      P3_est <- NA_real_

      striplet_est <- spatial_variances$se_N_striplet %||% NA_real_
      boxlet_est <- NA_real_

    } else {

      R2_est <- NA_real_
      R3_est <- NA_real_
      S1_est <- NA_real_
      S2_est <- NA_real_
      O1_est <- NA_real_
      O2_est <- NA_real_

      P2_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_P2 %||% NA_real_
      P3_est <- ds_results$analytical_variances$se_N_P3 %||% NA_real_

      striplet_est <- NA_real_
      boxlet_est <- spatial_variances$se_N_boxlet %||% NA_real_
    }

    standard <- get_bootstrap(
      region = region,
      population_description = dsm_results$population_description,
      sigma_hat = ds_results$sigma_hat,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      reps = bootstrap_reps
    )

    disc_density <- get_bootstrap_disc_density(
      region = region,
      population_description = dsm_results$population_description,
      sigma_hat = ds_results$sigma_hat,
      transect_type = points_or_lines,
      reps = bootstrap_reps
    )

    res_row <- tibble(
      iteration = b,
      scenario = paste("Scenario", i),
      N_hat = ds_results$N_hat,
      
      n_detections = nrow(survey_results$obsdata),

      se_R2 = R2_est,
      se_R3 = R3_est,
      se_S1 = S1_est,
      se_S2 = S2_est,
      se_O1 = O1_est,
      se_O2 = O2_est,
      se_P2 = P2_est,
      se_P3 = P3_est,

      se_striplet = striplet_est,
      se_boxlet = boxlet_est,

      se_boot_std = sd(standard$N_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
      se_boot_disc = sd(disc_density$N_hat, na.rm = TRUE)
    )

    list(
      row = res_row,
      bootstraps =
        if (b == outer_loop_reps)
          list(
            Standard = standard$N_hat,
            Discrete = disc_density$N_hat
          )
        else
          NULL
    )

  })

  list(
    summary = map_dfr(inner_results, "row"),
    bootstraps = map(inner_results, "bootstraps")
  )

}, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))

sim_results <- map_dfr(all_runs, "summary")

last_rep_bootstraps <- all_runs[[outer_loop_reps]]$bootstraps

plan(sequential)
```

```{r}
# Plotting Monte Carlo convergence 

# Custom function to calculate cumulative SD
cum_sd <- function(x) {
  # Start from 2 because you can't get an SD of a single number
  sapply(2:length(x), function(j) sd(x[1:j], na.rm = TRUE))
}

# Build plot data dynamically by looping through the saved list of the last iteration
plot_data <- bind_rows(lapply(1:num_scenarios, function(i) {
  
  boot_std <- last_rep_bootstraps[[i]]$Standard
  boot_disc <- last_rep_bootstraps[[i]]$Discrete
  
  tibble(
    replicate = 2:bootstrap_reps,
    Scenario = paste("Scenario", i),
    Standard = cum_sd(boot_std),
    Discrete = cum_sd(boot_disc)
  ) |> 
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(Standard, Discrete), 
      names_to = "Method", 
      values_to = "Running_SD"
    )
}))

# Generate the facet plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = replicate, y = Running_SD, color = Method)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~Scenario, scales = "free_y") + # 'free_y' allows each density to have its own y-axis scale
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Standard" = "blue", "Discrete" = "red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrap Convergence (Running SD)",
    subtitle = paste("Recorded from outer loop iteration", outer_loop_reps),
    x = "Bootstrap Replicate",
    y = "Cumulative Standard Deviation (SE of N_hat)",
    color = "Bootstrap Method"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
sim_summary <- sim_results |>
  group_by(scenario) |>
  summarize(
    mean_N_hat = mean(N_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_dets = mean(n_detections, na.rm = TRUE),
    true_SE = sd(N_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_R2 = mean(se_R2, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_R3 = mean(se_R3, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_S1 = mean(se_S1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_S2 = mean(se_S2, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_O1 = mean(se_O1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_O2 = mean(se_O2, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_P2 = mean(se_P2, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_P3 = mean(se_P3, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_striplet = mean(se_striplet, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_boxlet = mean(se_boxlet, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_boot_std = mean(se_boot_std, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se_boot_disc = mean(se_boot_disc, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(sim_summary) |>
  kable()
```


```{r}
desired_order <- c(
  "se_R2",
  "se_R3",
  "se_S1",
  "se_S2",
  "se_O1",
  "se_O2",
  "se_P2",
  "se_P3",
  "se_striplet",
  "se_boxlet",
  "se_boot_std",
  "se_boot_disc"
)

plot_dat <- sim_results |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(desired_order),
    values_drop_na = TRUE # This silently removes the 600 NA rows
  ) |>
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = desired_order))

plot_dat <- plot_dat |>
  group_by(scenario) |>
  mutate(true_ish_se = sd(N_hat))

ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = name, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = true_ish_se), color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(scenario)) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = TRUE) +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
# saveRDS(sim_results, file = "100_outer_250_inner")
```

note: All population size simulations were figured out on the uniform density, I will just assume that the other population sizes work when 10% is added.
